name: Memory Girl +
short_description: A tragic love story following Memori and her search for the missing "him."
detailed_description:
This game's heroine, Memori, awakens inside the data of a game, fully concious. She wanders through a fictional version of Tokyo, searching for clues to the whereabouts of "him."

The story progresses as Memori meets and gathers information from the quirky residents of Tokyo, including her best friend Messiah, her classmate who works at the arcade Cult, and the mysterious lone elementary student Lime.

Memori's choices will influence the direction of the story, and terrifyingly unexpected events may also occur…

Beautiful graphics and memorable music will immerse you in the mystery and horror of this game.

"The chapter… is replaying again…"

VA: Koyori Nohana

After "he," someone important to her (perhaps the player?), disappeared without a word, she apparently started searching the remaining game world for him.

A second-year student at High School A, located near Shibuya. In terms of personality, she's quiet and gentle. She apparently used to have a sharp and focused mind, but something happened to turn her forgetful and sloppy.

She's been friends with Messiah since they were young, and they often hang out together after school. While she's not the most proactive when it comes to making friends, whether it's because she has strong social skills or just because she's so easygoing, she gets along with most people easily. She can also be a bit odd, often leaving Messiah struggling to keep up with her.

VA: Emoko

Although you wouldn't expect it from her eye-catching fashion choices, she's a diligent and caring person who often finds herself dragged around by the forgetful Memori.

Because she's so kind, she frequently hides her true feelings out of consideration for others.

She loves cute, trendy things and checks social media daily to stay on top of what's popular.

Prior to high school, she was a plain girl who wore long skirts and kept her hair its natural black. She was obsessed with anime and manga, but discovering a stylish and inspiring internet idol motivated her to study fashion, and she made her high school debut as you see her now.

VA: Nitto Takamine

He's a laid-back, hands-off person who'll never reject anyone who approaches him, but he'll never chase anyone who avoids him either. This makes him seem more mature than most of his peers. After school, he puts on piercings, glasses, and a maid uniform, which he even wears at his job at the arcade. No one really knows why he walks around in this strange outfit.

He's close friends with Michiaki, and the two are constantly together while at school. He doesn't often join in on his friends' antics, but he does get a kick out of watching from the sidelines.

Recently, he's become concerned about Memori, and he's started talking to her at school in secret. This is because seeing her so despondent after losing someone important to her has made him worry about how her mental health might worsen in the future, and he feels obligated to reach out to help if she breaks down completely.

His real name is Ruto Kato.

VA: Yuri Sano

He's outgoing, cheerful, and full of curiosity. Because of his honesty and openness, he's bad at lying, and he can't keep a secret for long.

He has a variety of hobbies, including video games, manga, sports, and watching movies. Recently, he's gotten into digging up obscure B movies at rental video shops.

He and Cult are good friends and constant companions. However, ever since Cult started talking to Memori, he's had the sense that something terrible is going to happen, so he drops by Cult's job every day to keep an eye on him.

He might seem like a stalker, but he does it out of sincere concern.

VA: Narumi Aisaka

As kind and warm-hearted as a saint, and beautiful too, there's no shortage of students who worship her. She has a great memory and knows the faces and names of nearly all of the students. Because of her easy, peaceful, drama-free life, she has a fondness for gossip and crazy stories.

She especially loves stories about the darker side of humanity (all the better if they're nonfiction).

VA: Yayoi Yoi (Vtuber)

He's reserved, cautious, and shy, but once he determines that someone is kind, he quickly opens up, showing some of the trusting naivety you'd expect from a kid of his age.

He seems to be waiting for his mom to come pick him up after school.

VA: Edo

He's friendly, cheerful, and caring in a big sisterly sort of way, but despite his feminine demeanor and looks, he still considers himself to be a man.

He likes to take care of others, so whenever Memori, one of his regulars, pops in, he lets her have a meal or dessert on the house. He's on the cutting edge of trends, and he's into beauty and makeup. On his days off, he seems to keep up with Korean idols.

Not much is known about his background, but he has a lot of life experience. He's also bisexual, and has over 100 previous partners. He seems to have a long-distance boyfriend right now, but no one is sure.

VA: Baru

He seems somewhat suspicious at first glance, but he's actually friendly, kind, and chill.

He runs the occult zine "Onimaru's Mystery & Horror Stories" by himself as a hobby, posting issues online and advertising it by discretely putting up posters in out-of-the-way places around the city. Due to his constant efforts, the zine is gaining fans at a steady rate, and he dreams of turning it into a nationwide phenomenon.

He loves ghost stories, science fiction, horror, and other dark topics, often scouring the internet for new tales and concepts.

VA: Yotaro Uchimura

He wears a pale purple bear mask, so his real face is hidden.

He acts as if he's known Memori and Messiah for a while despite only just meeting them for the first time.

He appears to be interested in Memori, but any attempts to pry further result in him dodging the question with jokes.

  • An emotional story

  • High-quality voice acting

  • Warm and stylish graphics

  • A collab between Room97 and the creator as the theme song

*cream△ is the licensor of this game. For inquiries about the product, please contact Champloo Games.

supported_languages:
Japanese*, English, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese
*languages with full audio support
